name: Source Fidelity review for Mitochondrial Delivery And Transplantation bundle
summary: The staged coverage-repair records are faithful to the cited source boundaries. PMID 42171348 is represented as mouse xenogeneic mitochondrial transplantation evidence for selected age-associated phenotypes with donor-quality, donor-equivalence, and long-term safety caveats. PMID 41986718 is represented as MitoCatch targeted-delivery platform evidence in human and mouse cell systems plus mouse injury models, not treated-human aging efficacy. The interventions, studies, findings, and outlook update preserve those limits closely enough for editorial approval review.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged xeno-MT finding describes mouse evidence for selected age-associated phenotypes and mitochondrial functional readouts, with efficacy tied to donor mitochondrial integrity.
findings.why_it_matters: This matches the PubMed abstract and avoids over-reading the source as a human aging, lifespan, durable healthspan, or broadly donor-equivalent result.
findings.recommended_action: No source-fidelity revision required. Preserve the mouse-only, donor-quality, donor-equivalence, and long-term safety caveats through approval.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged MitoCatch finding limits the claim to targeted mitochondrial delivery in human and mouse cell systems plus in vitro and mouse injury-model rescue.
findings.why_it_matters: The source supports platform plausibility and disease-model rescue, but not treated-human aging efficacy or older-adult functional benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No source-fidelity revision required. Keep the platform, cell-system, and injury-model caveats in the finding and outlook support map.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged outlook adds the two primary coverage-repair sources while leaving recent reviews and reproductive ART context out of the public support map.
findings.why_it_matters: This keeps source use aligned with the track taxonomy and avoids moving reproductive, review-only, or adjacent disease-context evidence into the LEV-relevant support set.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required. Keep excluded review and reproductive-context records as coverage-assessment context rather than public evidence records.
